title: The Sandwich Shop on the Corner
summary: A London boy chats with the friendly shop owner while ordering his usual sandwich for lunch.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: Hello, Mr. Patel! Is my usual ready?

body.speaker: Mr. Patel
body.text: Hello, Liam! Cheese and tomato on brown bread, no butter?

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: Yes, please. And one packet of crisps.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: Vinegar, please. They are my favourite.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: A small orange juice, please. The cold one.

body.speaker: Mr. Patel
body.text: Here you go. Three pounds fifty altogether.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: Here is four pounds. Keep the change.

body.speaker: Mr. Patel
body.text: That is very kind. Thank you, young man.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: How is your dog, Mr. Patel?

body.speaker: Mr. Patel
body.text: Old Toby is fine. He sleeps under the counter all day.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: Can I say hello to him?

body.speaker: Mr. Patel
body.text: Of course. He likes you. He always wakes up when you come in.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: Hi Toby! Good boy. See you tomorrow, Mr. Patel!

body.speaker: Mr. Patel
body.text: See you tomorrow, Liam. Have a good lunch.

annotations.exampleEn: Is my usual ready? — món quen của tôi xong chưa?
She bought brown bread for the sandwich.
He opened a packet of crisps.
Salt and vinegar crisps are popular.
Five apples and one bread is three pounds altogether.
Here is ten pounds. Keep the change.
Excuse me, young man, can you help?
The shop owner stood behind the counter.
The baby wakes up at seven.

grammarNotes.title: Ordering food politely
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi gọi đồ ăn ta thường dùng 'I'll have …' hoặc '…, please.' Ví dụ: 'Vinegar, please.' / 'A small orange juice, please.' Người bán hay hỏi lựa chọn bằng 'Salt or vinegar?' / 'Drink?' (câu hỏi rất ngắn).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: When ordering food, use 'I'll have …' or '…, please.' For example: 'Vinegar, please.' / 'A small orange juice, please.' Shop owners often offer choices in very short questions: 'Salt or vinegar?' / 'Drink?'

grammarNotes.title: Frequency adverbs: always, all day
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trạng từ chỉ tần suất giúp nói thói quen: 'He sleeps under the counter all day.' / 'He always wakes up when you come in.' 'Always' thường đứng trước động từ chính (wakes), còn 'all day' đứng cuối câu.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Frequency adverbs describe habits: 'He sleeps under the counter all day.' / 'He always wakes up when you come in.' 'Always' usually goes before the main verb (wakes); 'all day' goes at the end.

translationVi: Liam: Chào chú Patel! Món thường lệ của cháu có chưa ạ? Chú Patel: Chào Liam! Phô mai và cà chua trên bánh mì lúa mì, không bơ phải không? Liam: Vâng ạ. Cho cháu thêm một gói khoai tây chiên nữa. Chú Patel: Vị muối hay vị giấm hôm nay? Liam: Vị giấm ạ. Cháu thích nhất món đó. Chú Patel: Lựa chọn hay đấy. Thức uống thì sao? Liam: Cho cháu một chai nước cam nhỏ ạ. Loại lạnh ấy. Chú Patel: Của cháu đây. Tổng cộng ba bảng năm mươi. Liam: Đây là bốn bảng. Chú giữ tiền thừa nhé. Chú Patel: Cháu tốt quá. Cảm ơn chàng trai. Liam: Chú Toby khỏe không chú? Chú Patel: Lão Toby vẫn ổn. Cả ngày nó ngủ dưới quầy. Liam: Cháu chào nó được không ạ? Chú Patel: Được chứ. Nó thích cháu lắm. Lần nào cháu vào nó cũng thức dậy. Liam: Chào Toby! Ngoan nào. Hẹn gặp chú ngày mai, chú Patel! Chú Patel: Hẹn gặp lại nhé Liam. Ăn ngon miệng.

options: Ham and cheese on white bread
Cheese and tomato on brown bread, no butter
Egg and cheese on brown bread
questions.explanation: 'Cheese and tomato on brown bread, no butter?'

questions.prompt: What kind of crisps does Liam choose?

questions.explanation: 'A small orange juice, please. The cold one.'

questions.explanation: 'He sleeps under the counter all day.'
'He always wakes up when you come in.'

questions.prompt: What is the relationship between Liam and Mr. Patel?
options: A regular customer and the shop owner
questions.explanation: Liam has a 'usual' order and they greet each other warmly.
It is the total for everything.

questions.prompt: What is the feeling at the shop?
questions.explanation: They know each other's names, the dog, and chat kindly.
questions.hint: Think about how the two people speak.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Liam and Mr. Patel know each other well. Why are small local shops important for people in big cities?
